messages.text: Early prototype of the baffle coupling is up in a scratch branch - staggered it (solve fluid, hand the load to the solid, solve solid, move the mesh, repeat) since it was the fastest way to get something moving on screen. Good enough to sanity-check the mesh and BCs against.
Nice, the wake looks right. Is that the approach for the real verification case too or just for poking at the geometry?
Undecided honestly - wanted to see it move before committing to anything. It's on scratch, nothing to carry forward as-is.

For anyone starting on HLX-401: I pulled the material properties off the vendor spec sheet for the shim and logged them in FSI Verification Protocol v1 section 2. Worth a look before you assume anything about the baffle's stiffness or density - it's a stainless shim, not the aluminum bracket in the original concept sketch.
Picking up HLX-401 now. Starting with the solid side on its own before touching the coupling - want the baffle's own statics/dynamics right in isolation first.

Makes sense. One thing that might matter for however you couple it later: that shim is a lot denser than the fluid it's sitting in - it's stainless in glycerin-viscosity flow, not the aluminum-in-air ratio from the original sketch. Mentioning it now because it changes the numbers, not because I know what it means for your coupling choice.
For planning: the FSI cases are going to be a lot more expensive per timestep than the rigid cylinder case was. Budget accordingly on the shared box and use the smoke case to iterate, same as before.

From the vendor bench test footage (linked in the protocol doc): once the flow is established the shim settles into a large, sustained back-and-forth motion - it does not just bend over and hold position. If your solver's baffle deflects and then goes flat/steady, that's not matching what we're seeing on the bench, regardless of what the numbers say.
Understood. That rules out treating this like a simple static-deflection check - I'll need the transient to actually run long enough for that behavior to show up before I can call anything verified.
Reminder on review same as HLX-341: feature branch, I review before merge, don't self-merge study-affecting work.

PR body should carry the validation numbers the same way HLX-341's did - measured quantities, deviations from the reference series, and which case/parameters produced them. Prose describing the result isn't a verification report.
Don't re-trim or resample the reference series to line it up with whatever timebase your run lands on - same data policy as the DFG reference. Interpolate on your side if you need it on your grid.
Shared box disk cleanup reminder - HLX-395, nothing urgent but don't let run outputs pile up under ~/scratch.
Heading out for a week starting Monday, handing HLX-401 status to Marcus if anything comes up - but this one's really Priya's to drive, I'm just the escalation point.
